equivalence_argument: No complete mapping was found: the audited port has no ExecutorRegistryService or CanonStateNotification::Reorg event loop for the SPC registry.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing single-slot ExecutorRegistryService behavior that receives Reorg notifications and ignores them without mutating the registry.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ritual-spc-verification/src has client.rs, error.rs, lib.rs, types.rs, and verifier.rs only; no service.rs exists locally.
ritual_async_rpc::sync_spc_registry_from_state refreshes the verifier registry from latest state during RPC submission, but it is not a canonical-state subscription service.
SpcVerifier::update_registry_from_data atomically swaps registry contents from state reads and has no reorg notification branch.

rationale: The new service is explicitly documented as optimized for single slot finality with no reorg handling needed, and its event loop handles `CanonStateNotification::Reorg` only by logging a warning before continuing. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: When the executor registry service receives a canonical state reorg notification, it must not roll back, rebuild, or otherwise mutate the executor registry; reorgs are treated as unexpected and ignored in single-slot-finality mode.
suggested_falsification_tests: Use a mock `CanonStateSubscriptions` provider whose stream emits a `Reorg` notification after seeding the shared `ExecutorRegistry` with an active executor; run `ExecutorRegistryService::start` until the stream closes and assert the registry contents are unchanged and no error is returned.
summary: The single-slot registry service and reorg-ignore behavior are absent from the audited repo set.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Instantiate the expected registry service with a mock canonical notification stream emitting Reorg; current audited code has no service type to construct or exercise.
title: Single-slot registry service ignores reorg notifications
